input.initialPrompt:
I have an HTTP-based C# MCP server built with ASP.NET Core.
How do I write integration tests that test the full MCP protocol
over HTTP without spinning up a real server?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to write integration tests for a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server built with ASP.NET Core, specifically testing the full MCP protocol over HTTP without spinning up a real server.

This is a knowledge/advice question rather than a task to perform on their codebase. I can answer this from my knowledge of ASP.NET Core testing patterns and MCP protocol.

The standard approach for testing ASP.NET Core HTTP endpoints without a real server is to use `WebApplicationFactory<T>` from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`, which creates an in-memory test server. This gives you a real HTTP pipeline but without network overhead.


I should walk through a practical example showing how to set this up for MCP protocol testing.
